factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields falling on Fed dovish repricing. FXStreet reports bond yields declining as inflation concerns ease and Kevin Warsh's tough talk paradoxically lowered yields; lower real yields are structurally bullish for gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy Pivot
factors.summary: Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari now projects only ONE rate hike in 2026 (down from prior expectations), citing doubts over US-Iran peace deal and AI spending concerns. This dovish shift removes a major headwind for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)
factors.summary: Greenback ended mixed on Friday and weakened alongside falling Treasury yields. FXStreet wrap notes USD momentum softened on Core PCE data; weaker dollar supports gold strength.

factors.name: Inflation Data (PCE)
factors.summary: Core PCE data came in softer than expected, triggering yield declines and equity weakness. Cooler inflation removes disinflation fears and supports dovish Fed narrative, gold-positive.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran)
factors.summary: US conducted retaliatory strikes on Iran after drone attack on cargo ship Ever Lovely in Strait of Hormuz. Elevated tensions typically support safe-haven demand (bullish gold), but oil fell hard (-8.5% weekly), suggesting risk-off without traditional flight-to-safety intensity.

factors.name: Equities & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Nasdaq down 4.6% for the week; major tech sell-off driven by AI spending ROI doubts and Oracle's worst week since 2001 dot-com. Risk-off broadly, but equities weakness coincides with yield collapse—mixed signal for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI)
factors.summary: Crude fell to $69.23, down 42.6% from $119.48 peak despite geopolitical tension. Oil weakness typically disinflationary; supports lower rate expectations but removes inflation-hedge component of gold narrative.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No recent CB buying data in news window. Structural bid from PBoC, RBI, Turkey remains in place but not materially shifted by this week's events.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU flow data in news window. Insufficient information to assess institutional positioning shift.

factors.name: Silver Price Action
factors.summary: Silver (XAG/USD) collapsed nearly 10% weekly, breaking below $60.00 for first time since December 2025 rally began. Weakness in silver typically signals risk-off liquidation, which can drag gold despite safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Upcoming NFP (July)
factors.summary: US jobs report is the major event ahead in the next 72h. Strong NFP could reinvigorate 'Fed on hold' narrative; weak data would confirm dovish turn and further support gold.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied +1.89% over 24h and +0.33% in 4h on the back of a dovish Fed repricing (Kashkari's one-hike projection, softer PCE, Warsh jawboning yields lower) and broad US Treasury yield declines. Risk-off sentiment from the tech sell-off and silver's -10% weekly collapse created initial safe-haven demand, but the move has already materialized—gold is in Phase 2 with +1.89% intraday gain. Geopolitical tensions (US-Iran strikes) add marginal support but are being offset by oil's 42.6% collapse, which removes inflation-hedge premium. The bias remains bullish on structural lower-real-yield story, but momentum is showing early signs of exhaustion.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (Non-Farm Payroll) release likely early next week — will test whether dovish Fed pivot is validated or if labor market strength forces rate-hike repricing
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI (next week) — key inflation/growth indicators; weak data = more dovish, strong data = re-hawkish risk
Eurozone inflation releases (next week) — ECB policy trajectory affects EUR/USD and broader DXY
Bank of England commentary & BoJ guidance — yen carry unwind risk if BoJ hikes further; USD/JPY near 162.00 is critical
FOMC minutes or Fed speaker schedule (if any this week) — any retreat from Kashkari's dovish tone would flip bias bearish
screening.risk_warning: If NFP comes in hot (>200k) or ISM Manufacturing surprises to upside, the 'one hike in 2026' narrative will be questioned and real yields could rebound sharply, cutting gold's legs out. Similarly, if US-Iran tensions escalate into broader conflict (offshore oil supply shock), oil re-rallies and inflation expectations spike—bullish for yields, bearish for gold. Equities finding a bid after this week's rout would also trigger a tactical pullback in gold's safe-haven premium. Finally, if silver stabilizes and reverses, it would confirm the current gold rally is not just liquidation-driven, validating the upside—lack of silver confirmation is a subtle warning.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already +1.89% in 24h; wait for pullback to $4,070–$4,080 support (Friday's 4h lows) before adding longs. If 4h close holds above $4,095, watch for test of $4,110–$4,120 resistance next week. For shorts, invalidation is a break above $4,130; do not chase downside until either NFP event risk passes or yields rebound materially (back above 4.2% on 10y nominal).

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: US Treasury yields falling sharply as investors trim hawkish Fed bets post-PCE (per FXStreet 19:54). Lower nominal yields with stable inflation expectations = lower real yields, structurally supportive for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Greenback weakening; FX strategists note USD momentum softened on PCE data (OCBC, FXStreet 21:58). Weaker dollar is directly bullish for gold priced in USD.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Shift
factors.summary: Fed Chair Warsh talking tough on inflation but markets pricing in LOWER rate hikes this year (Kashkari now projects one hike vs prior expectations); dovish tilt emerging. Reduces near-term tightening pressure.

factors.name: Inflation Data (PCE)
factors.summary: PCE print came in cool enough to ease hawkish concerns; no hot surprise reported. Market relief visible in bond yields collapsing and equities rotation into healthcare (safer havens).

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran Escalation)
factors.summary: US struck Iran in retaliation for drone/ship attack in Strait of Hormuz; fragile ceasefire tested (Al Jazeera multiple reports 00:00–04:13). Uncertainty/safe-haven demand typically supports gold. Oil initially up but settled lower ($69.23), limiting inflation spiral fears.

factors.name: Equity Market Weakness
factors.summary: NASDAQ down 4.60% for the week (5 consecutive down days); tech capitulation and AI spending doubts domino (MarketWatch 21:16). Risk-off regime favors gold as safe-haven; flight to quality ongoing.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Oil settled at $69.23 Friday (third consecutive weekly loss); Strait of Hormuz attacks contained via ship traffic resumption (Kriskey commentary). Not triggering stagflation fears; mutes inflation upside risk for gold.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Buying
factors.summary: No fresh flow data in news window. Structural CB bid ongoing but not highlighted; insufficient detail to score.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts (US Jobs Report)
factors.summary: US jobs report due this week (likely Friday); significant NFP miss or beat could shift Fed rate-cut odds and real yields. Event risk remains, capping near-term conviction.

screening.narrative: Gold is benefiting from a confluence of lower real yields, USD weakness, and equity risk-off after PCE cooled Fed hawkish bets and NASDAQ fell 4.6% for the week. US-Iran escalation adds safe-haven bid. However, the move is already underway (+1.89% in 24h, +0.08% in 4h), putting price action in early Phase 2; rally is nascent but not yet extended. Upcoming US jobs report and continued equity volatility are key near-term wildcards that could snap conviction either way.
catalysts_ahead: US Non-Farm Payroll (NFP) – likely Friday this week; critical for Fed rate-cut trajectory
US jobless claims (weekly) – ongoing, shapes labor-market narrative
Geopolitical escalation updates: Iran retaliation risk, Strait of Hormuz disruption severity, Israel-Lebanon framework enforcement
Fed speakers (Kashkari, Waller, Williams) – any fresh dovish/hawkish commentary shifts rate-cut odds
FOMC meeting minutes or next scheduled decision – could reset inflation/rate expectations
screening.risk_warning: If NFP surprise comes in HOT (significantly above expectations), Fed will slow rate-cut pace, real yields will rise, and gold will face headwinds. Alternatively, if equity bounce is sharp (VIX collapses), risk-on flows could reverse safe-haven demand. US-Iran ceasefire restoration would also reduce geopolitical premium. Watch DXY closely: if dollar reverses higher on hawkish surprise, gold downside is unprotected.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (+1.89% already captured); enter on pullbacks to $4,085–$4,095 (support zone near recent lows) rather than chasing higher. NFP Friday is a major event risk—consider waiting for post-data stabilization before new long entries to avoid whipsaw.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US escalation)
factors.summary: US struck Iran on Jun 26–27 after Iranian attack on cargo ship; fragile ceasefire at risk. VP Vance warned 'violence will be met with violence' and IRGC stated future responses will be 'broader'. Safe-haven bid for gold typically follows escalation.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No TIPS data in news window; no recent inflation surprise to reprrice real yields. Cannot establish strong bullish signal without this anchor.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Greenback ended mixed on Jun 26 after PCE data; DXY momentum softened per OCBC commentary on USD/SGD easing. No clear directional signal from FX.

factors.name: Inflation Prints (PCE, Core PCE)
factors.summary: PCE data released Jun 26 but not detailed in headlines; FedSpeak mentions 'core PCE' influencing sentiment but no actual print cited. Insufficient detail to score directionally.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy & FedSpeak
factors.summary: Fed Chair Warsh talking tough on inflation per Jun 26 report; Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari now projects one rate hike in 2026, shifting from dovish lean. Hawkish repricing headwind for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI closed Jun 26 at $69.23 (third consecutive weekly loss); bounced modestly in after-hours on Iran strike news, but overall downtrend. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations, headwind for gold safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No recent PBoC, RBI, or ECB gold purchase data in this news window; no structural bid signal detected.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No GLD, IAU holdings data provided in news window; cannot assess institutional positioning.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Action
factors.summary: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq all closed lower Jun 26; Nasdaq down 4.6% for the week, worst week in months. Tech selloff on AI spending concerns dominates; when equities rally, gold often weakens.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: DXY ended mixed but overall USD momentum softened; Bitcoin stuck below $63K per news. US dollar typically offers stronger safe-haven appeal in crisis; dollar strength would compete with gold bid.

factors.name: Recent Price Action
factors.summary: Gold +1.89% in 24h, +0.08% in 4h; move is already underway, suggesting phase 2 / early phase 3 of a bounce rather than fresh phase 1 setup.

factors.name: Venezuela Earthquakes & Disaster
factors.summary: Twin earthquakes killed 900+ in Venezuela; disaster relief underway but no direct impact on macro gold drivers (inflation, yields, DXY). Minor geopolitical noise.

screening.narrative: Gold is on a short-term bounce (+1.89% in 24h) driven by Iran-US escalation fears, which typically trigger safe-haven demand. However, headwinds are significant: Fed talk is turning hawkish (Kashkari now projects rate hike), tech equity selloff is broad and equity safe-haven flows may compete with gold, and oil is falling (reducing inflation narrative). The move is already in progress (Phase 2–3), meaning early-stage upside potential is limited. Price action suggests a tactical bounce rather than sustained bullish setup unless real yields fall materially or geopolitical risk spikes further.
catalysts_ahead: US Jobs Report (NFP) – expected early Jul, will reset rate-hike probability
Further Iran-US escalation or ceasefire confirmation – could trigger safe-haven bid or relief rally
Fed speakers (Powell, Williams, Waller) if scheduled – any hawkish lean pressures gold
Next CPI/PCE release – will reprrice real yields and Fed policy odds
Equity market stabilization – tech rally would compete with gold safe-haven flows
screening.risk_warning: If Fed rhetoric remains hawkish, real yields stay elevated, and tech stocks stabilize (reversing safe-haven demand), gold could roll over below $4,100. Conversely, if Iran-US tensions dramatically escalate or an unexpected inflation surprise emerges, gold breaks above $4,135. Current move is extension; be cautious of late entries into extended bounces.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already underway (Phase 2–3); wait for pullback to $4,090–$4,095 support or extended run to $4,120 resistance before considering new entry. Avoid chasing on open.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran escalation)
factors.summary: US strikes Iran after alleged ceasefire violation in Strait of Hormuz; IRGC retaliates targeting US military sites. Escalating Middle East tensions historically boost safe-haven demand for gold, especially with shipping/trade route disruption risks.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in news window; nominal 10y yields likely softening modestly on flight-to-safety and Fed dovish pivot narrative, but PCE headline softened recently per prior context. Real yields remain key headwind if they stay elevated.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY momentum softened on core PCE data per FXStreet commentary; SGD gaining on USD weakness suggests broad dollar easing. Weaker USD is structurally bullish for gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Fed Chair Warsh Signal
factors.summary: Warsh's 'tough talk on inflation' is helping coax Treasury yields lower (per MarketWatch); markets pricing lower rate-hike expectations. Lower nominal yields and potential for real yield compression favour gold.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent PCE)
factors.summary: Core PCE headline softened recently (reference to PCE in SG/Korea context); no fresh US CPI/PCE in last 24h, so inflation surprise tailwind is fading. Kashkari now projects only one hike this year, signal of disinflation narrative.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equity Weakness
factors.summary: Tech stocks down 4.6% for the week; NASDAQ down 5 straight days; AI spending concerns escalating (Oracle worst week since 2001). Risk-off environment typically supports gold as safe-haven despite some USD strength.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI settled $69.23 on Friday, posting third-straight weekly loss despite US-Iran strike; oil extended declines post-attack. Geopolitical premium is muted, suggesting markets already priced conflict risk or expect demand destruction.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying / ETF Flows
factors.summary: No fresh CB or ETF flow data in news window; longer-term structural bid from PBoC/RBI remains in place but not an immediate catalyst.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: 24h change +1.73% is a solid intraday move in gold's favour; 4h change -0.09% shows recent pullback/consolidation. Move is underway but not extended—typical Phase 2 zone.

factors.name: Bias History Consistency
factors.summary: Prior three reads (00:05, 04:52, 07:48 UTC) all BUY bias 5-6/10, no confirmation yet. Today's geopolitical spike + yield/dollar softening reinforce prior setup; this is a fresh confirmation catalyst, not a reversal.

screening.narrative: Gold has benefited from a confluence of risk-off sentiment (tech rout, AI spending fears), Fed dovish repricing (Warsh inflation talk, Kashkari one-hike projection), and fresh geopolitical premium from US-Iran escalation. The 1.73% 24h rally is real but not extended—consolidation into resistance is likely. Dollar weakness and lower real yields are structural tailwinds, but oil's muted response suggests markets may already be digesting Middle East risk. Watch for any de-escalation headlines or US economic surprises that could flip the sentiment back to risk-on.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (likely next Friday, 2026-06-26/27 window depending on week)
Fed Chair Warsh speeches / forward guidance (monitoring tone shift)
Further US-Iran developments (ceasefire hold/break critical)
Equity open Monday (risk-on reversal could weaken gold if tech bounces)
PCE/CPI print (if hot, pressures gold; if cool, supports it)
Oil price follow-through (if geopolitical premium re-inflates, crude strength aids gold)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) US-Iran tensions de-escalate sharply → risk-on rally, gold pullback; (2) Fed delivers surprise hawkish pivot (e.g. Q3 rate hike signalled) → real yields spike, gold sold; (3) Tech/equities bounce hard on AI narrative recovery → risk appetite resets, DXY strengthens, gold pressured; (4) Oil rallies >10% on supply disruption fears → inflation expectations reignite, real yields rise, gold underperforms. Monitor 4h close above $4,110 for potential PHASE_3 exhaustion.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 consolidation; wait for a retest of $4,080–$4,090 support with volume confirmation before fresh longs. If price holds above $4,100 into Monday open and equities remain weak, target $4,120–$4,140 resistance. Do not chase the current spike; geopolitical premiums tend to fade on headlines.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS or real yield data in news window. Prior PCE data showed core PCE momentum softening (per OCBC FX commentary on USD easing), which is mildly supportive for lower real yields, but lack of explicit 10y TIPS update prevents high conviction.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: No new FOMC decision or Powell/Waller/Williams commentary in last 24h. Market is in inter-meeting period with no scheduled speakers mentioned. Absence of hawkish surprise supports mild bid for gold but does not drive conviction.

factors.name: US Nominal Yields & 2s10s Curve
factors.summary: No explicit 10-year yield or curve data published in news window. Backdrop remains: softer PCE reducing near-term rate-hike expectations, but no material inversion or steepening catalysts evident.

factors.name: Inflation Prints & Expectations
factors.summary: Core PCE noted as having softened (supporting weaker USD per OCBC), but no fresh CPI/PCE/PPI releases in the ~24h window. Lack of hot inflation surprise removes near-term downside driver for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY) & Major Pairs
factors.summary: FXStreet reports USD ended mixed but still higher for the week; OCBC notes USD/SGD eased on softer PCE momentum. Mixed DXY direction limits support for gold strength; USD strength still a headwind despite softening.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tensions (Middle East)
factors.summary: US struck Iran targets on 2026-06-27 after alleged ceasefire violation near Strait of Hormuz; Iran claims MoU violation and threatens broader retaliation. Escalating US-Iran military exchanges and shipping disruption risk are classic safe-haven drivers for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI settled Friday at $69.23 (below $70), posting third-straight weekly loss despite US strike on Iran. Oil weakness typically reduces inflation fears and is mixed for gold; shipping premiums narrowed recently but could rise again. No strong directional bias.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: Major indices closed modestly lower Friday with Nasdaq leading declines; VIX and credit spreads not explicitly quoted. Tech rotation and Oracle weakness noted but no panic-level equity selloff. Safe-haven demand present but not acute.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & CB Buying
factors.summary: No GLD, IAU, or central bank (PBoC, RBI) buying data in news window. Lack of institutional flow confirmation limits conviction on structural bid.

factors.name: Physical Demand Seasonality
factors.summary: Late June; not a major seasonal demand period for gold (Indian weddings summer lull, no Chinese NY). No seasonal tailwind.

screening.narrative: Gold has rallied +1.73% over 24h (+0.09% flat in last 4h), landing at $4,096.30 after a modest intraday pullback. The backdrop is mixed: escalating US-Iran military exchanges (strikes on both sides, MoU threatened) provide classic safe-haven support, but oil weakness (WTI sub-$70 for a third week), mixed USD action (higher on week but softening on benign PCE), and absence of fresh inflation or Fed catalysts limit conviction. Prior BUY bias calls (5-6/10 confidence) have not triggered confirmations, suggesting the market is still digesting forces rather than committing to a strong directional trend.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran ceasefire status / further military escalation (next 24-48h; high risk of additional strikes or rhetoric)
Oil market reaction to shipping/geopolitical developments (Strait of Hormuz closure risk)
Fed speakers (next week; none explicitly noted for next 48h)
Weekly initial jobless claims (Thursday AM, typically low-impact for gold but adds dataflow)
PCE final read or Core PCE update if revised (no date specified in window; if due soon could shift real yields)
screening.risk_warning: Bias would be invalidated by: (1) sudden US-Iran ceasefire breakthrough (removes safe-haven bid), (2) sharp DXY rally back above recent highs (USD strength typically corrodes gold), (3) hotter-than-expected inflation print driving real yields higher (classic gold headwind), or (4) equity market stabilization without geopolitical escalation (reduces risk-off flows).
screening.entry_guidance: Phase 2 move is modest and unconfirmed; avoid chasing. Better entry on pullback to $4,070–$4,080 support with a retest of geopolitical risk, or on a fresh breakout above $4,120 resistance WITH fresh catalysts (escalated Iran/Israel tensions or major USD weakness). Monitor oil and USD closely; no high-conviction entry justified at current levels.
